input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue with keyboard handling in .NET 10. This is a known breaking change — `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated/removed because Android itself deprecated `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE`. In .NET 10 (MAUI), the recommended approach changed.

Let me provide a concise, accurate answer based on my knowledge of .NET MAUI evolution.

In .NET 9+, Android deprecated `adjustResize`. .NET MAUI moved toward using `Edge-to-Edge` display and the `WindowInsets` API. In .NET 10, the recommended approach is to use the `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` or handle insets manually.


So the main solutions are `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` which is enabled by default on Android but can be configured, and using Edge-to-Edge layouts with proper inset handling to ensure the keyboard doesn't obscure input fields.

For a chat interface specifically, I'd structure it with a `CollectionView` for messages, an `Entry` or `Editor` at the bottom, and wrap everything in a `Grid`. The tricky part with .NET 10 is that MAUI switched to edge-to-edge mode by default on Android, which changes how `adjustResize` behaves, so I need to handle `WindowInsets` properly or rely on the built-in keyboard scrolling behavior instead.
